title: How Does an Abundant Leader Decide?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for exploring what leading from abundance means in the specific, practical decisions of a Monday morning
excerpt: A journal prompt set from the Day 81 theme of a 12-week money programme. The prompts open with the quality of abundance-based leadership, then narrow to the specific ways it shows up in decisions, conversations, and presence.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 81 journal prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want to understand what it would mean in practical terms to lead your decisions and interactions from a place of abundance.

content.text: A journal prompt set from the Day 81 theme of a 12-week money programme. Day 81: see yourself as an abundant leader. The prompts look at what leading from abundance means in specific, observable terms: how decisions are made, how others experience your presence, where you can step up.
New to journal prompts as practice? Read
What does it mean to lead from abundance rather than striving or scarcity?
Write what the difference looks like in practice, not in the abstract.
Who have I seen lead this way?
Name a specific person. Write what they do differently.
What does an abundant leader do on Monday morning?
Make it concrete. What are the first three decisions of the morning?
Where can I step up and model calm, generous certainty this week?
Name the specific situation. Write what you would do differently from there.
Who in my life have I inspired recently, knowingly or not, simply by being steady?
Find one example. Write it out.
Adapted from the Day 81 journal prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
